page: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpldsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor architecture operating at 3.4 GHz. The system is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes a bundled local storage capacity of 2835 GB and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory subsystem performance. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core tests, Passmark memory scores, and Passmark database operations. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes also rank in the top tier. Conversely, single-core performance remains in the average tier, as demonstrated by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core benchmarks.

Due to its low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, this server is not designed for memory-intensive applications. However, the high physical core count and top-tier memory bandwidth make it highly resource-dense for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive RAM footprints. It is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that leverage local storage, while showing weaker performance on large-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processors. Configured with 96.0 GB of memory and 2835 GB of local storage, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads with low memory requirements. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside average single-core execution speeds. The server is optimized for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and database workloads that benefit from high-density physical cores and fast local storage, though it exhibits weaker performance in large-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 48 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 96 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48plds_v6 is an ARM-based server with 48 dedicated cores, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based 48-core virtual machine featuring high-density physical compute, fast local storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.

96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
2835 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage support
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database operations
